commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(agents): cap bootstrap-cache at 64 entries with LRU eviction
target.body:
Fixes #88148

**Behavior addressed:** `src/agents/bootstrap-cache.ts` stored one workspace bootstrap snapshot per session key in a `Map` with no upper bound. A long-running gateway accumulates one resident entry per distinct session key, producing unbounded memory growth over time. This patch caps the cache at 64 entries with LRU-style eviction (delete-and-reinsert refreshes the position on access; the oldest insertion-ordered key is evicted when the cap is reached). Successful cache hits and the per-turn content refresh are unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node.js v22.x, HEAD `a71b7f300d75eeb35689794681312909e25f8853` based on `origin/main` `e6782254e45752eb1ba51df0b0968c5f12d74826`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-cache.test.ts \
  --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000
```

Three new regression tests drive the real cache via `getOrLoadBootstrapFiles`: insert 65 distinct session keys and assert the cache stays capped at 64; assert the oldest key is evicted (next access misses and hits the loader); assert an accessed key is refreshed so it survives a later eviction.

**Evidence after fix:**
```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  9 passed (9)
```

Before/after on the "stays bounded under churn" test (eviction temporarily disabled to reproduce current main's unbounded behavior, then restored): with the unbounded cache the same 65-key churn leaves the cache at 65 entries:
```
AssertionError: expected 65 to be 64 // Object.is equality
- 64
+ 65
 ❯ src/agents/bootstrap-cache.test.ts:141  expect(getBootstrapCacheSizeForTest()).toBe(64)
```
With the eviction patch the cache stays at 64 and all 9 tests pass.

**Observed result after fix:** Under churn of arbitrarily many distinct session keys the cache size never exceeds 64; the oldest entry is evicted and recently-accessed keys are kept hot.

**What was not tested:** No live multi-hour gateway run was performed; the proof exercises the real cache module directly (mocked workspace loader, in-process `Map`), which is exactly where the unbounded retention lives.
target.author: alix-007
